title: feat: implement idempotent payment retry logic
description:
Adds idempotency key support to the payment processing pipeline. This ensures that retried payments due to network failures don't result in duplicate charges.

## Changes
- Added `IdempotencyKeyMiddleware` to the request pipeline
- New `payment_attempts` table for tracking retry state
- Updated `PaymentProcessor.execute()` to check for existing attempts
- Added comprehensive test coverage for retry scenarios

Linked to PAY-189
author: sarah.chen

reviewers.body: LGTM! The idempotency key logic is solid. One minor suggestion on the retry backoff.
The middleware looks great but I think we need to handle the edge case where the idempotency key expires mid-transaction. See inline comments.

diff_summary:
diff --git a/src/middleware/idempotency.py b/src/middleware/idempotency.py
new file mode 100644
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/middleware/idempotency.py
@@ -0,0 +1,85 @@
+class IdempotencyKeyMiddleware:
+    async def process_request(self, request, payment_id):
+        existing = await self.store.get(request.idempotency_key)
+        if existing:
+            return existing.response
+        ...

diff --git a/src/processors/payment.py b/src/processors/payment.py
--- a/src/processors/payment.py
+++ b/src/processors/payment.py
@@ -45,6 +45,15 @@ class PaymentProcessor:
+    async def execute_with_retry(self, payment, max_retries=3):
+        for attempt in range(max_retries):
+            try:
+                return await self.execute(payment)
+            except TransientError:
+                await asyncio.sleep(2 ** attempt)
+        raise MaxRetriesExceeded(payment.id)

title: fix: resolve race condition in concurrent refund processing
description:
Fixes a critical race condition where two concurrent refund requests for the same transaction could both succeed, resulting in double refunds.

## Root Cause
The `RefundService.process()` method was not acquiring a distributed lock before checking the refund status.

## Fix
- Added Redis-based distributed locking around refund processing
- Added database-level unique constraint on `(transaction_id, refund_type)`
- Added integration tests for concurrent refund scenarios
author: marcus.johnson

reviewers.body: Great catch on the race condition. The distributed lock approach is the right call here.
Approved. The integration tests are thorough.

diff_summary:
diff --git a/src/services/refund.py b/src/services/refund.py
--- a/src/services/refund.py
+++ b/src/services/refund.py
@@ -22,8 +22,16 @@ class RefundService:
-    async def process(self, transaction_id, amount):
-        refund = await self.repo.get_pending(transaction_id)
+    async def process(self, transaction_id, amount):
+        async with self.lock_manager.acquire(f'refund:{transaction_id}'):
+            refund = await self.repo.get_pending(transaction_id)
+            if refund and refund.status == 'completed':
+                raise RefundAlreadyProcessed(transaction_id)

title: chore: upgrade stripe SDK to v12 and update webhook handlers
description:
Upgrades Stripe SDK from v10 to v12. Updates deprecated webhook event handlers to use the new API surface.

Breaking changes in Stripe v12:
- `stripe.Charge` replaced by `stripe.PaymentIntent`
- Webhook signature verification updated
- New event types for payment lifecycle
author: alex.kumar
diff_summary:
diff --git a/src/integrations/stripe_client.py b/src/integrations/stripe_client.py
--- a/src/integrations/stripe_client.py
+++ b/src/integrations/stripe_client.py
@@ -1,4 +1,4 @@
-import stripe  # v10
+import stripe  # v12
 
@@ -15,10 +15,12 @@ class StripeClient:
-    def create_charge(self, amount, currency, source):
-        return stripe.Charge.create(
+    def create_payment(self, amount, currency, payment_method):
+        return stripe.PaymentIntent.create(
             amount=amount,
             currency=currency,
-            source=source,
+            payment_method=payment_method,
+            confirm=True,
         )

title: feat: add payment analytics dashboard API endpoints
description:
New REST API endpoints for the payment analytics dashboard. Provides aggregated metrics for payment volumes, success rates, and processing times.

Endpoints:
- `GET /api/v1/analytics/volume` - Payment volume over time
- `GET /api/v1/analytics/success-rate` - Success/failure rates
- `GET /api/v1/analytics/latency` - P50/P95/P99 processing latency
author: priya.patel

+router = APIRouter(prefix='/api/v1/analytics')
+

title: fix: handle null currency code in international payments
description:
Fixes a bug where international payments with missing currency codes would throw an unhandled NullPointerException instead of returning a proper validation error.

Linked to PAY-210 (P0 bug).
author: james.wilson

reviewers.body: Looks good. Let's get this merged ASAP for the P0 fix.
Approved. Good test coverage on the edge cases.

diff_summary:
diff --git a/src/validators/payment.py b/src/validators/payment.py
--- a/src/validators/payment.py
+++ b/src/validators/payment.py
@@ -30,6 +30,10 @@ class PaymentValidator:
     def validate_international(self, payment):
+        if not payment.currency_code:
+            raise ValidationError(
+                'currency_code is required for international payments'
+            )

title: feat: redesign checkout flow with multi-step wizard
description:
Complete redesign of the checkout flow using a multi-step wizard pattern.

Steps:
1. Cart review
2. Shipping information
3. Payment method
4. Order confirmation

Includes animated transitions, form validation, and progress indicator.
author: elena.rodriguez

reviewers.body: The wizard flow is much better UX. Approved!
Accessibility: the progress indicator needs aria-labels and the step transitions need to manage focus correctly for screen readers.

title: fix: cart total calculation wrong with discount codes
description: Fixes incorrect total when applying percentage-based discount codes to carts with mixed tax rates. The discount was being applied after tax instead of before.
author: priya.patel

reviewers.body: Good fix. The test cases cover the edge cases well.
Approved. Verified the calculation matches the spec.

title: feat: add Apple Pay and Google Pay integration
description: Integrates Apple Pay and Google Pay as payment options in the checkout flow. Uses the Payment Request API for a unified interface.
author: james.wilson

reviewers.body: Payment Request API integration looks clean. Approved.

title: chore: migrate from Webpack to Vite
description:
Migrates the build system from Webpack 5 to Vite for faster development builds and HMR.

- Dev server startup: 12s -> 0.8s
- HMR update: 2s -> 50ms
- Production build: 45s -> 18s
author: alex.kumar

reviewers.body: Build times are dramatically better. Nice work!

title: fix: shipping address form loses state on browser back navigation
description: Fixes an issue where the shipping address form loses all entered data when the user navigates back from the payment step. Adds session storage persistence for form state.
author: elena.rodriguez
